demos.task_description: Summarize this bug report for a non-technical project manager.
demos.input_data:
Bug ID: #4821
Severity: High
Description: NullPointerException thrown in UserAuthService.validateToken() when the JWT expiry claim is missing. Stack trace shows the crash originates at line 87 of AuthService.java. Reproducible 100% of the time when a token without an 'exp' field is passed to the /api/refresh endpoint.
demos.reasoning: I need to apply the task requirements carefully to produce a high-quality output.
demos.output: Users cannot refresh their login sessions when their security token is missing an expiry date, causing the app to crash every time this occurs.

demos.task_description: Explain this code to a beginner.
demos.reasoning: I need to apply the task requirements carefully to produce a high-quality output.
demos.output: This is like a recipe that asks 'to make a dish for n people, make one batch for n-1 people and one for n-2 people, then combine them.' For 0 or 1 people you already know the answer, so you stop there. The result follows the Fibonacci sequence: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8...
